qa_pairs.question: What happens to the Application Binary Interface (ABI) if one only uses `-std=c++98` or `-std=c++11` without explicitly setting `_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI`?
qa_pairs.answer: If one only uses `-std=c++98` or `-std=c++11` without specifying `_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI`, the ABI does not differ, and the compiler will use the default (i.e., newer) ABI.

qa_pairs.question: How does setting `_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI` to `0` or `1` affect the ABI used in compilation when combined with `-std=c++98` or `-std=c++11`?
qa_pairs.answer: When `_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI` is set to `0` (old ABI) or `1` (new ABI) along with `-std=c++98` or `-std=c++11`, it causes either the old or new ABI to be used, respectively.

qa_pairs.question: When using GCC v5.1 or higher, what compiler option is required to link an application with binaries that were compiled using the old ABI?
qa_pairs.answer: To link to binaries compiled using the old ABI when using GCC v5.1 or higher, one will need to specify `-D_GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI=0` when compiling the C++ code.
